source_jobs.title: Security Compliance Officer
source_jobs.description:
Overview

We are on a mission to ensure everyone has access to medical expertise, no matter where they are.

Half the world still lacks access to quality healthcare. Even in advanced systems, outcomes are uneven, and clinicians are overwhelmed. Medical knowledge grows faster than human capacity can keep up.

Corti exists to close that gap. Our AI platform expands access to medical expertise, reducing errors, restoring time to clinicians, and making care more affordable, accessible, and human again.

There is no quality healthcare without a quality dialogue, and no reliable AI without a strong foundation. Help us build both.



Why Corti?

Corti is the frontier lab for clinical-grade AI. We build world-leading models and infrastructure that power healthcare's most ambitious software - from EHR vendors and virtual care platforms to the health systems that care depends on. Today, Corti serves over 100 million patients annually, including the NHS.

Our flagship model, Symphony, is trained and validated on real clinical data from real patient interactions, and powers the clinical and administrative work that healthcare runs on.

We power the builders who are redefining how healthcare works, from startups creating new patient experiences to enterprises modernizing the systems that care depends on.

If you believe that AI purpose-built for medicine will define the next century of healthcare, you belong at Corti.



The Role

Corti is seeking a Security Compliance Officer to ensure we continue to meet and exceed our rigorous standards for data privacy, security, and regulatory compliance, particularly as it pertains to managing sensitive patient information. In this role, you will manage our adherence to essential frameworks such as ISO 27001, SOC 2, Cyber Essentials, and BSI C5. These frameworks are fundamental to safeguarding sensitive data, as they establish strict controls on data privacy, risk management, and region-specific compliance.



The ideal candidate will play a critical role in implementing, monitoring, and refining our compliance strategies to meet evolving regulatory requirements. By leading our compliance efforts, you’ll help build trust with clients, partners, and regulatory bodies, demonstrating our commitment to responsible data handling and supporting the long-term security and sustainability of our operations.



The Security Compliance Officer would work closely with other Governance & Market Access team members, platform team, Technical teams, including management, to develop, implement and maintain compliance policies and procedures, as well as ensure company adherence to them.



What you’ll be doing:

 - Own and maintain Corti security governance model across ISO 27001, ISO 27017, SOC 2 and other relevant frameworks, including a clear control inventory and ownership map.

 - Translate frameworks and customer requirements into concise policies, playbooks, checklists and acceptance criteria that fit naturally into tech docs, release processes and change management.

 - Plan, manage and follow up on internal and external security audits and assessments, address any areas of non compliance and communicate status and findings to leadership, auditors and teams.

 - Drive the use of compliance automation tools (e.g. Drata) and internal scripts, and monitor indicators such as device compliance, policy acceptance, training completion and access reviews, coordinating remediation with the responsible teams.

 - Maintain a live security risk register, including risk acceptance, mitigation plans and regular reviews with Product, Platform and Governance team members.

 - Partner with Platform and other engineers to ensure that policies and control objectives are reflected in CI or CD pipelines, Infrastructure as Code and cloud configuration baselines, manage incidents and review security impactful changes at a governance level so they remain aligned and auditable.

 - Act as a trusted advisor on secure ways of working an

source_jobs.title: Digital Natives Healthcare GTM Leader (UK & Ireland)
source_jobs.description:
Overview

We are on a mission to ensure everyone has access to medical expertise, no matter where they are.

Half the world still lacks access to quality healthcare. Even in advanced systems, outcomes are uneven, and clinicians are overwhelmed. Medical knowledge grows faster than human capacity can keep up.

Corti exists to close that gap. Our AI platform expands access to medical expertise, reducing errors, restoring time to clinicians, and making care more affordable, accessible, and human again.

There is no quality healthcare without a quality dialogue, and no reliable AI without a strong foundation. Help us build both.



Why Corti?

Corti is the frontier lab for clinical-grade AI. We build world-leading models and infrastructure that power healthcare's most ambitious software - from EHR vendors and virtual care platforms to the health systems that care depends on. Today, Corti serves over 100 million patients annually, including the NHS.

Our flagship model, Symphony, is trained and validated on real clinical data from real patient interactions, and powers the clinical and administrative work that healthcare runs on.

We power the builders who are redefining how healthcare works, from startups creating new patient experiences to enterprises modernizing the systems that care depends on.

If you believe that AI purpose-built for medicine will define the next century of healthcare, you belong at Corti.



The Role

We are seeking an exceptional and forward-thinking individual to join our team as the Digital Natives Healthcare GTM Leader. You cannot do this job well if you’re not obsessed with elevating the UK healthcare to higher levels, and you want to have a direct impact. Maybe you have skin in the game?

Your job is to get Corti’s AI into the hands of as many motivated partners as possible, so they can help their customers across the UK and Irish healthcare sector reach new heights. Every time our partners sell our products, a doctor somewhere gets a little more time for patients, increases the quality of the consultation and spends a little less fighting paperwork. This position requires strong business acumen, excellent communication skills, and a track record of successful partnership development, but even more importantly, it requires the right kind of person with grit, determination, and an intense longing to make a difference.




What you'll be doing:

 - Build high quality relationships with key C-level executives.

 - Sell directly to customers too, and you often join sales calls to drive home the point.

 - Lead the identification and evaluation of potential partnership opportunities.

 - Drive negotiations and contract discussions with partners, working closely with legal and finance teams to ensure favorable terms.

 - Train and facilitate partners, reps and sales people in how to pitch Corti.



Required Skills

 - 5+ years working with digital-native and tech-forward healthcare software companies in the UK

 - Experience driving adoption of API-first, platform or AI infrastructure products

 - Understanding of how modern healthtech products are built and scaled for provider markets

 - Comfortable engaging founders, product leaders, and engineering teams

 - Technically fluent; comfortable discussing APIs, integrations, and developer workflows — familiarity with JavaScript, Python, C#, or similar backend languages is a plus

 - Familiarity with healthcare AI concepts and platform economics (e.g., tokens, usage-based pricing, credits, accuracy and safety considerations)
   

What you bring:

 - Strong hunter–farmer mindset with a track record of growing strategic accounts

 - Proven success selling enterprise B2B platform, or API-based products

 - Consistent quota achievement as an individual contributor or team lead

 - Collaborative and trusted partner across founders, product teams, and commercial stakeholders

 - High ownership and willingness to step in beyond role boundaries to drive outcomes

 - Exp

source_jobs.title: Technical Growth Marketer
source_jobs.description:
Overview

We are on a mission to ensure everyone has access to medical expertise, no matter where they are.

Half the world still lacks access to quality healthcare. Even in advanced systems, outcomes are uneven, and clinicians are overwhelmed. Medical knowledge grows faster than human capacity can keep up.

Corti exists to close that gap. Our AI platform expands access to medical expertise, reducing errors, restoring time to clinicians, and making care more affordable, accessible, and human again.

There is no quality healthcare without a quality dialogue, and no reliable AI without a strong foundation. Help us build both.



Why Corti?

Corti is the frontier lab for clinical-grade AI. We build world-leading models and infrastructure that power healthcare's most ambitious software - from EHR vendors and virtual care platforms to the health systems that care depends on. Today, Corti serves over 100 million patients annually, including the NHS.

Our flagship model, Symphony, is trained and validated on real clinical data from real patient interactions, and powers the clinical and administrative work that healthcare runs on.

We power the builders who are redefining how healthcare works, from startups creating new patient experiences to enterprises modernizing the systems that care depends on.

If you believe that AI purpose-built for medicine will define the next century of healthcare, you belong at Corti.




THE ROLE

We are seeking a technically curious, execution-focused Growth Marketer to sit at the center of Corti’s growth engine.

This role is for someone who can read an API doc, spin up a landing page, and write a launch post that developers actually want to read. You are technical enough to understand what we build, creative enough to make people care, and pragmatic enough to ship instead of overthinking.

You will own the surfaces and campaigns that drive developer awareness, acquisition, and activation. That includes product launches, landing pages, demand generation, developer-facing content, and growth analytics. You will move fast across channels and formats, with real ownership over what ships and how it performs.




WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING:




PRODUCT LAUNCHES AND LANDING PAGES

 - Design and build landing pages, launch assets, and promotional materials (often coding or prototyping yourself)

 - Collaborate on the end-to-end build and launch process for new products, features, and platform updates

 - Coordinate cross-functionally with product, engineering, and design to bring launches to market

 - Write launch content and developer-facing announcements that inspire our audience to dive in


DEMAND GENERATION

 - Plan and execute campaigns that drive signups, activations, and pipeline across paid, organic, and owned channels

 - Run conversion rate experiments across the signup and activation funnel

 - Manage paid campaigns and track performance against acquisition and activation targets


CONTENT AND SEO

 - Produce developer-facing content for blog, social, and community channels

 - Build and execute an SEO strategy targeting developers searching for healthcare AI tooling

 - Write technical blog posts, guides, and tutorials that position Corti as the default platform for healthcare AI development

 - Create sales enablement assets, templates, and collateral as needed


ANALYTICS AND REPORTING

 - Build dashboards and reports to track campaign performance, funnel metrics, and growth KPIs

 - Analyze user behaviour to identify activation patterns and conversion opportunities

 - Report regularly on what’s working, what’s not, and where to invest next

 - Use data to prioritise experiments and allocate resources across channels




WHAT YOU BRING:

You may have been a growth marketer at a developer tools company, a product marketer at a PLG SaaS business, or a growth generalist at a Series A-C startup who touched everything from paid ads to SEO to rapid prototyping.

Whatever your path, you have marketed a tec

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Overview

We are on a mission to ensure everyone has access to medical expertise, no matter where they are.

Half the world still lacks access to quality healthcare. Even in advanced systems, outcomes are uneven, and clinicians are overwhelmed. Medical knowledge grows faster than human capacity can keep up.

Corti exists to close that gap. Our AI platform expands access to medical expertise, reducing errors, restoring time to clinicians, and making care more affordable, accessible, and human again.

There is no quality healthcare without a quality dialogue, and no reliable AI without a strong foundation. Help us build both.



Why Corti?

Corti is the frontier lab for clinical-grade AI. We build world-leading models and infrastructure that power healthcare's most ambitious software - from EHR vendors and virtual care platforms to the health systems that care depends on. Today, Corti serves over 100 million patients annually, including the NHS.

Our flagship model, Symphony, is trained and validated on real clinical data from real patient interactions, and powers the clinical and administrative work that healthcare runs on.

We power the builders who are redefining how healthcare works, from startups creating new patient experiences to enterprises modernizing the systems that care depends on.

If you believe that AI purpose-built for medicine will define the next century of healthcare, you belong at Corti.



The Role

Are you a curious person, and interested in contributing to solving real problems in healthcare? Are you someone who loves the Golang programming language?

If so, we would love for you to become an integral part of a fun, autonomous, and team-oriented culture guided by shared values, a high bar for quality, and a willingness to experiment.

Join the Engineering team as a Senior Backend Engineer to be part of pioneering the development of healthcare products. Our team specifically focuses on developing software that assists the medical community within speech recognition and text generation. Your work will build the backend that helps the medics, nurses, and doctors learn from their calls to get better for each one. You will learn every day by working alongside experts in software engineering, machine learning, and data science—are you up for the adventure?



What you'll be doing:

 - Own the technical development of a product that differentiates Corti the most in the market

 - Hands-on development of an innovative solution where design choices matter

 - Collaborate across our product and platform teams to define and improve best practices across Corti

 - Work with colleagues in design and code reviews

 - Bridge technical communication between end-users, technical leads, and Corti engineers

 - Contribute to a strong culture of development at Corti through mentorship of developers of all levels



What you bring:

 - Proven experience as a backend developer

 - Mastery of Golang

 - Experience in ideating and implementing features with high complexity

 - Hands-on experience with cloud computing architecture and development

 - Deep experience with software and product development processes - you have seen where problems can arise, know how to deal with them, and how they can be proactively avoided

 - Experience running, monitoring, and operating REST, WebSockets, Kafka or an event-driven architecture

 - You know what it takes to make APIs more public & accessible

 - Experience with containers and orchestration tools

 - A passion for writing clean and efficient code

 - Excellent English verbal and written communication skills, including all-remote communication



Life at Corti

 - You will be reporting to the Senior Engineering Manager.

 - The position is full-time and starts as soon as possible.

 - Hybrid working environment in our Copenhagen Office

 - Equipment provided by Corti





Ready to dive into the world of Corti? Hit that 'Apply' button, and let's start working together on reshaping the d

source_jobs.title: Join Corti's Talent Community
source_jobs.description:
Overview

We are on a mission to ensure everyone has access to medical expertise, no matter where they are.

Half the world still lacks access to quality healthcare. Even in advanced systems, outcomes are uneven, and clinicians are overwhelmed. Medical knowledge grows faster than human capacity can keep up.

Corti exists to close that gap. Our AI platform expands access to medical expertise, reducing errors, restoring time to clinicians, and making care more affordable, accessible, and human again.

There is no quality healthcare without a quality dialogue, and no reliable AI without a strong foundation. Help us build both.



Why Corti?

Corti is the frontier lab for clinical-grade AI. We build world-leading models and infrastructure that power healthcare's most ambitious software - from EHR vendors and virtual care platforms to the health systems that care depends on. Today, Corti serves over 100 million patients annually, including the NHS.

Our flagship model, Symphony, is trained and validated on real clinical data from real patient interactions, and powers the clinical and administrative work that healthcare runs on.

We power the builders who are redefining how healthcare works, from startups creating new patient experiences to enterprises modernizing the systems that care depends on.

If you believe that AI purpose-built for medicine will define the next century of healthcare, you belong at Corti.



Looking to Launch Your Tech Career?

Start your journey with Corti, where innovation meets impact. Joining Corti isn't just about a job, it's about shaping the future of healthcare AI. We're expanding our team and inviting passionate individuals like you to be part of our mission.

Discover various roles and functions tailored to your skills and aspirations. Whether you're passionate about AI technology, machine learning, or software development, Corti offers diverse opportunities to grow and excel.

At Corti, you'll work alongside industry experts and innovators in a collaborative environment. Shape the future of healthcare through cutting-edge AI technologies and make a tangible impact on how data shapes our world.

Join Corti's Talent Community Today

Submit your interest to stay informed about Corti, early access to future opportunities, and more. Take your first step towards building a rewarding career in tech with Corti.

Ready to Shape the Future?

Explore career opportunities and join Corti's talent community today. Your journey towards a meaningful career in tech starts here.



Ready to dive into the world of Corti? Hit that 'Apply' button, and let's start working together on reshaping the dialogue in healthcare, making a real difference for millions of patient outcomes around the world.

🤝 Bringing in top talent from all backgrounds is crucial in our pursuit to improve the world of healthcare. We encourage applications from all people and do not discriminate based on race, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, and/or disability status.﻿

At Corti, experience comes in many forms, and we’re passionate about creating teams with a multitude of perspectives! If you believe your experience is close to what we’re looking for but not an exact match, we still hope you’ll consider applying!

 - Experience designing data storage solutions is a plus

 - Experience with audio streaming is a big plus

 - General knowledge of IP telephony, on a conceptual or protocol level would be favorable

source_jobs.title: Senior Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Overview

We are on a mission to ensure everyone has access to medical expertise, no matter where they are.

Half the world still lacks access to quality healthcare. Even in advanced systems, outcomes are uneven, and clinicians are overwhelmed. Medical knowledge grows faster than human capacity can keep up.

Corti exists to close that gap. Our AI platform expands access to medical expertise, reducing errors, restoring time to clinicians, and making care more affordable, accessible, and human again.

There is no quality healthcare without a quality dialogue, and no reliable AI without a strong foundation. Help us build both.



Why Corti?

Corti is the frontier lab for clinical-grade AI. We build world-leading models and infrastructure that power healthcare's most ambitious software - from EHR vendors and virtual care platforms to the health systems that care depends on. Today, Corti serves over 100 million patients annually, including the NHS.

Our flagship model, Symphony, is trained and validated on real clinical data from real patient interactions, and powers the clinical and administrative work that healthcare runs on.

We power the builders who are redefining how healthcare works, from startups creating new patient experiences to enterprises modernizing the systems that care depends on.

If you believe that AI purpose-built for medicine will define the next century of healthcare, you belong at Corti.



The Role

In this role, you will be shaping the next iteration of our multi-tenant Kubernetes setup. You will be helping us scale the infrastructure to handle over 100,000 concurrent streams.

Additionally, you will help our engineers become faster by creating solutions to improve their developer experience.



What you'll be doing:

 - Work closely with product and engineering teams to ensure that our systems are scalable, reliable and performant.

 - Design and implement automation tools and processes to improve the efficiency of our development and operations teams.

 - Design and architect our platform to scale as the company grows.

 - Create and improve the existing toolset for developers to improve their developer experience.

 - Contribute to a strong culture of development at Corti through mentorship and knowledge sharing of people inside as well as outside the team.



What you bring:

 - Proven experience in a Senior Platform Engineer or Senior DevOps role.

 - Experience with containerization and orchestration tools like Docker and Kubernetes. Such as architecting a multi-tenant cluster, implementing GitOps, horizontal/vertical scaling and running a fault resilient cluster

 - Experience with cloud platforms such as Azure, AWS or GCP. Such as deploying infrastructure-as-code, configuring cloud resources to meet security and compliance standards

 - Strong knowledge of programming languages such as Golang and Python.

 - Experience with managing and operating distributed, stateful systems on Kubernetes such as Redis Clusters, PostgreSQL clusters or Apache Kafka

 - Experience writing CI/CD pipelines such using Github Actions

 - Experience with the usage and development of Kubernetes Operators

 - Excellent English verbal and written communication skills, including all-remote communication

Life at Corti

 - You will be reporting to the Lead Platform Engineer.

 - The position is full-time and starts as soon as possible.

 - Hybrid working environment in our Copenhagen office

 - Equipment provided by Corti



Ready to dive into the world of Corti? Hit that 'Apply' button, and let's start working together on reshaping the dialogue in healthcare, making a real difference for millions of patient outcomes around the world.

🤝 Bringing in top talent from all backgrounds is crucial in our pursuit to improve the world of healthcare. We encourage applications from all people and do not discriminate based on race, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, and/or disability status.﻿

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer, Audio Processing
source_jobs.description:
Overview

We are on a mission to ensure everyone has access to medical expertise, no matter where they are.

Half the world still lacks access to quality healthcare. Even in advanced systems, outcomes are uneven, and clinicians are overwhelmed. Medical knowledge grows faster than human capacity can keep up.

Corti exists to close that gap. Our AI platform expands access to medical expertise, reducing errors, restoring time to clinicians, and making care more affordable, accessible, and human again.

There is no quality healthcare without a quality dialogue, and no reliable AI without a strong foundation. Help us build both.



Why Corti?

Corti is the frontier lab for clinical-grade AI. We build world-leading models and infrastructure that power healthcare's most ambitious software - from EHR vendors and virtual care platforms to the health systems that care depends on. Today, Corti serves over 100 million patients annually, including the NHS.

Our flagship model, Symphony, is trained and validated on real clinical data from real patient interactions, and powers the clinical and administrative work that healthcare runs on.

We power the builders who are redefining how healthcare works, from startups creating new patient experiences to enterprises modernizing the systems that care depends on.

If you believe that AI purpose-built for medicine will define the next century of healthcare, you belong at Corti.



The Role

We are looking for an engineer passionate about building the next generation of high-scale audio processing infrastructure for communication centers that form a vital part of our critical infrastructure, including emergency dispatch centers and hospitals. You will be responsible for integrating AI capabilities into environments dominated by network protocols like SIP and SIPREC, ensuring robust and scalable performance at the heart of life-critical operations.



What you'll be doing:

 - Architect and maintain systems processing over 100,000 calls per day.

 - Develop foundational infrastructure using network protocols such as SIP and SIPREC.

 - Integrate AI capabilities into low-latency, high-throughput audio processing pipelines.

 - Collaborate with the Platform Engineering team with a commitment to engineering excellence to ensure system reliability and scalability.

 - Hands-on development of an innovative solution where design choices matter

 - Work with colleagues in design and code reviews

 - Contribute to a strong culture of development at Corti through mentorship of developers of all levels



What you bring:

 - Master of Golang

 - Deep understanding of network protocols, specifically SIP and SIPREC

 - Proven track record of building and scaling foundational infrastructure

 - Experience in ideating and implementing features with high complexity

 - Hands-on experience with cloud computing architecture and development

 - Experience running, monitoring, and operating REST, WebSockets, Kafka or an event-driven architecture

 - Experience with containers and orchestration tools

 - A passion for writing clean and efficient code

 - Experience with WebSocket, gRPC and CockroachDB is a significant plus.

 - Experience with real-time audio processing and WebRTC is a significant plus.

 - Excellent English verbal and written communication skills, including all-remote communication



Life at Corti

 - You will be reporting to the Director of Platform Engineering.

 - The position is full-time and starts as soon as possible.

 - Hybrid working environment in Copenhagen.

 - Equipment provided by Corti



Ready to dive into the world of Corti? Hit that 'Apply' button, and let's start working together on reshaping the dialogue in healthcare, making a real difference for millions of patient outcomes around the world.

Half the world still lacks access to quality healthcare. Even in advanced systems, outcomes are uneven, and clinicians are overwhelmed. Medical knowledge grows faster than human capacity can keep up.

There is no quality healthcare without a quality dialogue, and no reliable AI without a strong foundation. Help us build both.

Corti is the frontier lab for clinical-grade AI. We build world-leading models and infrastructure that power healthcare's most ambitious software - from EHR vendors and virtual care platforms to the health systems that care depends on. Today, Corti serves over 100 million patients annually, including the NHS.

Our flagship model, Symphony, is trained and validated on real clinical data from real patient interactions, and powers the clinical and administrative work that healthcare runs on.

We power the builders who are redefining how healthcare works, from startups creating new patient experiences to enterprises modernizing the systems that care depends on.

If you believe that AI purpose-built for medicine will define the next century of healthcare, you belong at Corti.



The Role

As Staff Platform Engineer, you will define the technical vision and architecture for the future of our infrastructure. Beyond the platform itself, you will serve as a multiplier across engineering, driving the frameworks that enable teams to ship faster and with greater confidence.



What you'll be doing:

 - Work closely with product and engineering teams to ensure that our systems are scalable, reliable and performant.

 - Design and implement automation tools and processes to improve the efficiency of our development and operations teams.

 - Design and architect our platform to scale as the company grows.

 - Create and improve the existing toolset for developers to improve their developer experience.

 - Elevate engineering capability through mentorship, knowledge sharing, and leadership both within and beyond the Platform team.

 

What you bring:

 - Extensive years of experience in a Senior Platform Engineer or Senior DevOps role.

 - Mastery experience with containerization and orchestration tools like Docker and Kubernetes. Such as architecting a multi-tenant cluster, implementing GitOps, horizontal/vertical scaling and running a fault resilient cluster

 - Experience with cloud platforms such as Azure, AWS or GCP. Such as deploying infrastructure-as-code, configuring cloud resources to meet security and compliance standards

 - Strong knowledge of programming languages such as Golang and Python.

 - Experience with managing and operating distributed systems such as CockroachDB, ElasticSearch or Apache Kafka.

 - Experience writing CI/CD pipelines such as Azure DevOps, Jenkins, or Github Actions.

 - Experience with the usage and development of Kubernetes Operators



Ready to dive into the world of Corti? Hit that 'Apply' button, and let's start working together on reshaping the dialogue in healthcare, making a real difference for millions of patient outcomes around the world.

source_jobs.title: Senior Communications Manager
source_jobs.description:
Overview

We are on a mission to ensure everyone has access to medical expertise, no matter where they are.

Half the world still lacks access to quality healthcare. Even in advanced systems, outcomes are uneven, and clinicians are overwhelmed. Medical knowledge grows faster than human capacity can keep up.

Corti exists to close that gap. Our AI platform expands access to medical expertise, reducing errors, restoring time to clinicians, and making care more affordable, accessible, and human again.

There is no quality healthcare without a quality dialogue, and no reliable AI without a strong foundation. Help us build both.



Why Corti?

Corti is the frontier lab for clinical-grade AI. We build world-leading models and infrastructure that power healthcare's most ambitious software - from EHR vendors and virtual care platforms to the health systems that care depends on. Today, Corti serves over 100 million patients annually, including the NHS.

Our flagship model, Symphony, is trained and validated on real clinical data from real patient interactions, and powers the clinical and administrative work that healthcare runs on.

We power the builders who are redefining how healthcare works, from startups creating new patient experiences to enterprises modernizing the systems that care depends on.

If you believe that AI purpose-built for medicine will define the next century of healthcare, you belong at Corti.



The Role

As Senior Communications Manager, you'll work directly with the VP of Brand & Communications to bring Corti's narrative strategy to life across markets, channels, and formats.

This isn't a traditional PR role. Yes, you'll manage agency relationships and drive media coverage. But you'll also be a creative partner: brainstorming campaign ideas, building assets, working with data to find unexpected angles, and helping us do things that make people pay attention.

We have a clear strategic framework and a network of agencies across key markets. What we need is someone who can operate across all of it - turning proof points into compelling stories, lifting launches beyond the basics, and making our agencies more impactful by equipping them with outstanding content and infectiously good ideas.

Corti has four strategic pillars. Every product development, research output, and customer win is a proof point for one of them. Your job is to work with internal stakeholders to surface those proof points through earned media in the markets we need to reach - primarily Germany, France, and the UK. You’ll do this by working with our agency network and directly with journalists.

You'll also be the face of communications to our commercial team - the person they come to when a customer win, partnership, or deployment deserves a story. Building that trust and rhythm is central to the role.

This role is ideal for someone who's done the traditional communications work and is ready for more creative latitude. You'll have real ownership, direct exposure to how narrative strategy gets built, and the chance to shape how a scaling healthcare AI company shows up in the world.



What you'll be doing

 - Lead our strategy and execution in Germany and France, contribute meaningfully to healthcare titles in the UK, and manage the agency relationships that support these markets. You'll localize narratives, co-create plans with agencies, and set a clear creative bar for the work they deliver.

 - Own partner and customer comms - work closely with commercial and partnership teams to turn customer wins, deployments, and partnerships into compelling proof points across markets.

 - Work across Corti's four strategic pillars to develop and execute comms that make each one tangible - through customer stories, campaign ideas, media angles, and creative assets.

 - Brainstorm and build - we want someone who can come up with a creative campaign concept in a meeting, then actually help make it happen (whether that's a data visualization, a launch asse

source_jobs.title: Senior MLOps Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Overview

We are on a mission to ensure everyone has access to medical expertise, no matter where they are.

Half the world still lacks access to quality healthcare. Even in advanced systems, outcomes are uneven, and clinicians are overwhelmed. Medical knowledge grows faster than human capacity can keep up.

Corti exists to close that gap. Our AI platform expands access to medical expertise, reducing errors, restoring time to clinicians, and making care more affordable, accessible, and human again.

There is no quality healthcare without a quality dialogue, and no reliable AI without a strong foundation. Help us build both.



Why Corti?

Corti is the frontier lab for clinical-grade AI. We build world-leading models and infrastructure that power healthcare's most ambitious software - from EHR vendors and virtual care platforms to the health systems that care depends on. Today, Corti serves over 100 million patients annually, including the NHS.

Our flagship model, Symphony, is trained and validated on real clinical data from real patient interactions, and powers the clinical and administrative work that healthcare runs on.

We power the builders who are redefining how healthcare works, from startups creating new patient experiences to enterprises modernizing the systems that care depends on.

If you believe that AI purpose-built for medicine will define the next century of healthcare, you belong at Corti.



The Role

We're looking for an MLOps Engineer to help ensure our machine learning models run reliably and add value in production. In this role, you'll focus on monitoring, maintaining, and integrating models into products, while also helping move models into production.



What you'll be doing:

 - Own and manage the full lifecycle of both ML models and core infrastructure – from development and deployment to monitoring and continuous improvement.

 - Build and maintain robust CI/CD pipelines for both software and ML workflows.

 - Ensure reliability, scalability, observability, and security of production systems and ML infrastructure.

 - Automate deployment, orchestration, and environment management using modern DevOps tooling.

 - Collaborate closely with software engineers, ML engineers, and product teams to bring ML-powered features to production.

 - Proactively detect, troubleshoot, and resolve infrastructure and model performance issues.

 - Stay up to date with industry best practices in DevOps, MLOps, and infrastructure engineering.

 - Document infrastructure, workflows, and operational procedures clearly and thoroughly.



What you bring:

 - Experience deploying machine learning models into production and managing their lifecycle

 - Experience implementing model governance, including versioning, monitoring, drift detection, and reporting

 - Familiarity with MLOps tools such as MLflow, Kubeflow, or DVC

 - Solid understanding of CI/CD systems (e.g., GitHub Actions, ArgoCD) and infrastructure-as-code tools (e.g., Terraform, Helm).

 - Familiarity with data engineering concepts such as ETL pipelines, data lakes, and large-scale batch/stream processing.

 - Experience mentoring or supporting colleagues to help them grow their technical skills

 - Proven experience in a senior-level DevOps, MLOps, or related infrastructure-focused engineering role.

 - Strong proficiency in Python.

 - Deep experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and container orchestration tools (Docker, Kubernetes).

 - Ability to design scalable, secure, and observable systems in fast-moving environments.

 - Strong debugging and problem-solving skills across distributed systems.

 - Excellent collaboration and communication skills, with experience working in cross-functional teams.

 - Understanding of security and compliance best practices for both software and ML systems.



Life at Corti

 - You will be reporting to the Director of Engineering

 - The position is full-time and starts as soon as possible.

 - Hybrid working en

notes: AI platform for medical expertise, multiple senior engineering roles
